title: Fix admin blog post save and publish functionality

requirements.text: Fix the backend blog post creation so that when an admin submits the Create Blog Post form, the post is correctly persisted in the canister stable storage and a valid post ID is returned.

quotes: admin part is not working properly my blog post are not getting saved or published please fix it

acceptanceCriteria: Submitting the Create Blog Post form successfully stores the post in the backend actor.
The returned post ID is valid and non-null.
The newly created post appears in the admin posts list after creation.

requirements.text: Fix the backend publish/unpublish logic so that toggling the published state of a blog post (via the publish button or checkbox) correctly updates the post's published flag in stable storage.

quotes: my blog post are not getting saved or published please fix it

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Publish on an unpublished post sets its published flag to true in the backend.
The post appears in the public published posts list after publishing.
Clicking Unpublish sets the published flag back to false and removes it from the public list.

requirements.text: Fix the CreateBlogPostPage form submission so that it correctly calls the backend createPost mutation, handles the response (including error states), and navigates back to the admin posts list on success.

quotes: my blog post are not getting saved or published please fix it

acceptanceCriteria: Submitting the Create Blog Post form invokes the backend createPost mutation.
On success, the user is redirected to the AdminPostsPage.
On failure, a visible error message is displayed to the admin.

requirements.text: Fix the AdminPostsPage publish button and the CreateBlogPostPage publish checkbox so that they correctly invoke the backend publish/unpublish mutations and reflect the updated state in the UI without requiring a full page reload.

quotes: my blog post are not getting saved or published please fix it

acceptanceCriteria: The publish/unpublish button on AdminPostsPage triggers the correct mutation.
The post's status updates optimistically or refetches after the mutation completes.
The publish checkbox on CreateBlogPostPage correctly sets the post's initial published state.

constraints: Do not change the existing blog post data schema in a way that breaks existing stored posts without providing a migration.
Immutable frontend paths (useInternetIdentity, useActor, main.tsx, ui components) must not be modified.
nonGoals: Adding new admin features beyond save and publish.
Changing the visual design of the admin pages.
Modifying comment or user profile functionality.
